message:
feat(fp-io): add to_excel sheet_name/index/index_label/header parity (frankenpandas-emw9)

Existing `write_excel` hard-coded the sheet name to rust_xlsxwriter's
default ("Sheet1") and always wrote an index column — pandas'
`DataFrame.to_excel(sheet_name=..., index=False, index_label=...,
header=False)` flags had no counterpart.

Adds `ExcelWriteOptions` and the pair:
- `write_excel_bytes_with_options(frame, options)`
- `write_excel_with_options(frame, path, options)`

Options mirror pandas:
- `sheet_name: String` — custom sheet name (default "Sheet1").
- `index: bool` — whether to emit the leading index column (default
  true).
- `index_label: Option<String>` — header for the index column when
  present; falls back to the frame's index name.
- `header: bool` — whether to emit the column-name row (default
  true). When false, data rows start at row 0.

The existing `write_excel_bytes` now delegates to the options path
with defaults, keeping byte-level compatibility for existing
callers (a test asserts `default` options produce identical bytes).

Five tests cover: custom sheet_name round-trip, index=false
shrinking the output width, index_label renaming the leading header,
header=false eliminating the header row, and default-path byte
equivalence.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
